title: Wholesome Smoky Yucatecan Fish Enchiladas
description: A smoky, flaky fish enchilada that blends Yucatecan flavors with a diabetic-friendly twist, served from a clay pot for a rustic touch.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat and sauté the garlic powder, cumin, and smoked paprika until fragrant.
Pour in the dry sherry and lime juice, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer for 3 minutes to reduce slightly.
Stir in the shredded low-fat cheddar until melted and smooth, then remove from heat.
Lay the toasted corn tortillas in a single layer at the bottom of a clay pot.
Arrange the flaky fish fillets over the tortillas, seasoning each piece lightly with salt and pepper.
Spoon the cheesy sauce evenly over the fish, covering all fillets completely.
Place the clay pot over a bain-marie, letting the double boiler gently heat the enchiladas for 15 minutes.
After 10 minutes, carefully remove the pot from the water bath and place it directly on a preheated oven set to 350°F to finish baking for an additional 5 minutes.
Garnish the finished enchiladas with chopped cilantro and diced red onion before serving.
Serve immediately with a side of fresh lime wedges if desired.
